### Post by blkno1 on 2006-06-24
I installed quake 4 and it runs fine.  The only problem I'm having is trying to figure out the screen resolutions.  Im running dual-monitors (twin-view) and when I run quake it spans both monitors, how can I get it to stay on just one.  Also its not running full screen, and I cant seem to change the res on the system options.

Here's my Xorg.conf

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"

	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

EndSection

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA Default Card"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
        Option      "TwinView"
        Option      "MetaModes" "1280x1024, 1280x1024"

EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"VX922"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA Default Card"
	Monitor		"VX922"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x640" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x640" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x640" "640x480"
	EndSubSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

### Post by slinkan on 2006-06-27
Hello blkno1, IIRC your problem is with the "MetaModes" option in xorg.conf, you need to specify a couple more than you have, including some with one of the monitors turned off (this is from mine):

```

Section "Screen"
    Identifier  "Screen 1"
    Device      "My Video Card"
    Monitor     "Dell D1626HT"
    DefaultDepth 24
    Option	"NvAGP" 	"1"
    Option	"RenderAccel" 	"1"
    Option	"CursorShadow"	"1"
    Option	"UseEdidFreqs"	"1"
    Option	"TwinView"	"1"
    Option	"SecondMonitorHorizSync"	"31-107"
    Option	"SecondMonitorVertRefresh"	"50-160"
    Option	"MetaModes"	"1600x1200,1600x1200; 1280x1024,1280x1024; 1024x768,1024x768; 800x600,800x600; 640x480,640x480; NULL,1024x768; NULL,800x600; NULL,640x480"

```
Each "couple" delimited by ; specifies resolution on monitor 0 and 1 respectively, and NULL means that the monitor won't be used.

Hope this helps

---

### Post by blkno1 on 2006-07-13
I simply copied your meta-mode settings and voila!  I can now play quake full screen.  Thanks for the info.

-bk
8)
